course_description:
This course provides you with the opportunity to critically evaluate published clinical research and reflect on your own clinical practice. Through guided research activities, you will explore topics of personal and clinical relevance. The course is intentionally flexible, allowing you to tailor your research to align with your individual clinical interests and experience.

As a core component of the Master of Medicine (Skin Cancer) program, this course highlights the importance of evidence-based decision-making. We hope you will find that, as you progress through this course, these essential principles are foundational to delivering quality patient care and add intellectual stimulation to your daily clinical practice. Whether interpreting literature to address clinical questions or undertaking original research, the ability to apply these principles is fundamental to skin cancer medicine.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

course_contacts.name: Medical School Student and Academic Adminsitration Team

timetable:
Additional timetable information
There is no timetable for this course, all material is provided online and the study modules can be completed online each week by you at your convenience.

learning_outcomes.description: Critically analyse and integrate information from a wide range of sources
Evaluate opinions, make decisions and to reflect using an evidence-based approach
Formulate and investigate problems, create solutions, innovate and improve current practices
Collect, analyse and organise information and ideas and to convey those ideas clearly and fluently, in both written and spoken forms
Apply critical reasoning to issues through independent thought and informed judgement
Identify clinical questions in your everyday clinical practice, and formulate these into answerable research questions
Develop the skills to critically evaluate a component of your own clinical practice to ensure that practice is supported by recognised quality standards and/or research evidence
Describe and explain the concepts of Evidence Based Medicine and appreciate their relevance to everyday clinical practice

assessment_summary.title: Critical appraisal of a scientific paper - Report
Critical appraisal of a scientific paper - oral presentation

assessment_details.title: Critical appraisal summary
assessment_details.task_description: You should choose and review a journal article from the list provided. The chosen article is to be scientifically analysed against your clinical practice background. A basic draft of the report will be submitted to complete the compliance hurdle. 1. Identify which journal article you will critique 2. 300-word summary of the Strengths and Limitations of the selected study. The purpose of the draft is to provide you with the opportunity to receive feedback prior to your final submission. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission instructions will be provided via Learn.UQ .
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
If you require an extension, please contact me via email and it will be considered.
Lauren Aoude l.aoude@uq.edu.au
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.title: Critical appraisal of a scientific paper - Report
assessment_details.task_description: You should choose and review a journal article from the list provided. They will assess the scientific quality of the article and write a critique of the key features of the manuscript. The report should critically evaluate key aspects such as the clarity and relevance of study aims, the suitability of the study design, the quality of the background and discussion and study limitations. The report will additionally discuss the clinical relevance of the paper's content to your area of work in clinical skin cancer medicine. Expected word count is maximum 1500 words. assessment_details.task_description: Prepare a 10 minute presentation. Create approximately 10 slides (1 slide per minute as a rough guide) to step through the key findings for of your critical appraisal. Record yourself presenting your appraisal. assessment_details.title: Project Plan for Literature Review
assessment_details.task_description: Using the knowledge and skills gained from this course as well as earlier courses in the Master program, you will prepare a literature review in an area relevant to skin cancer practice. You will select a topic from the list provided. The purpose of the draft is to provide you with the opportunity to receive feedback prior to your final submission. 1. Identify your topic for review 2. At a minimum, provide dot points for the key elements of the report 3. Include a basic reflection on the relevance to skin cancer practice Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Surgical SCARD Audit Report
assessment_details.task_description: You will be required to submit a clinical log via use of the SCARD database. The log requires a minimum of 50 cases. Proper collation and entry of data will make analysis easier. You will complete an audit of your own patients, including a written analysis (1500 words) comparing your results with those from the broader SCARD database of skin cancer practitioners The Introduction/rationale must include a description of your clinical practice. Although a detailed knowledge of statistics is not required, it is expected some basic analytical principles be employed when looking at the data. Analysis of raw data will be complete when accompanied by literature research, contemplation of how these results tabulate with your patient population and medical practice setup. Details will be made available on the course Learn.UQ site. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Literature Review
assessment_details.task_description: Clinical practice is built on a foundation of published knowledge. Reviewing published work is an essential part of professional development. The final assessment is the preparation of a literature review (maximum 2000 words) in an area relevant to skin cancer. You will select a topic from the list provided. You are to write a structured literature review using the framework provided. Details will be made available on the course Learn.UQ site. At the end of this course, students completing this task should be confident in preparing a journal article and to feel comfortable with the concept of possibly publishing future work. learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Course content (Reading) Course reading will include published articles, as well as online resources. Links to these resources will be available through Learn.UQ . These resources are designed to support the assessment tasks for this course. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

learning_activities.topic: Zoom Meetings Online tutorial meetings via Zoom will be held to discuss the course requirements as well as the upcoming assessment tasks. The meetings will be hosted by the course co-ordinators. The Zoom meeting schedule will be posted on Learn.UQ. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
